---
title: "Rhobots"
subtitle: "BERTopic-Style Topic Modelling in Pure R"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: cosmo
    css: rhobots_site.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        body { max-width: 960px; margin: auto; }
        /* ── Hero ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */
        .hero {
          background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e 0%, #16213e 50%, #0f3460 100%);
          color: white;
          padding: 3.5rem 2.5rem;
          border-radius: 12px;
          margin-bottom: 2.5rem;
          text-align: center;
        }
        .hero h2  { color: #e94560; font-size: 2.1rem; margin-bottom: 0.4rem; }
        .hero p   { font-size: 1.1rem; opacity: 0.9; max-width: 680px; margin: 0.6rem auto 0; }
        .hero .badges { margin-top: 1.4rem; }
        .badge {
          display: inline-block;
          background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
          border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.25);
          color: white;
          padding: 0.25rem 0.75rem;
          border-radius: 20px;
          font-size: 0.82rem;
          margin: 0.2rem;
        }
        /* ── Pipeline diagram ─────────────────────────────────── */
        .pipeline {
          display: flex;
          align-items: center;
          justify-content: center;
          flex-wrap: wrap;
          gap: 0;
          margin: 2rem 0 2.5rem;
        }
        .pipe-step {
          background: #f8f9fa;
          border: 2px solid #0f3460;
          border-radius: 10px;
          padding: 0.9rem 1.2rem;
          text-align: center;
          min-width: 130px;
        }
        .pipe-step .icon  { font-size: 1.6rem; display: block; }
        .pipe-step .label { font-size: 0.78rem; color: #0f3460; font-weight: 700;
                            text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; margin-top: 0.3rem; }
        .pipe-step .pkg   { font-size: 0.72rem; color: #6c757d; margin-top: 0.15rem; }
        .pipe-arrow { font-size: 1.5rem; color: #e94560; padding: 0 0.5rem; }
        /* ── Resource cards ───────────────────────────────────── */
        .card-grid {
          display: grid;
          grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(270px, 1fr));
          gap: 1.2rem;
          margin: 0 0 2.5rem;
        }
        .card {
          border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
          border-radius: 10px;
          padding: 1.5rem;
          box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);
          transition: box-shadow 0.2s;
          text-decoration: none !important;
          color: inherit;
          display: block;
        }
        .card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 18px rgba(15,52,96,0.15); }
        .card .card-icon  { font-size: 2rem; margin-bottom: 0.6rem; }
        .card h4 { color: #0f3460; margin: 0 0 0.5rem; font-size: 1.05rem; }
        .card p  { font-size: 0.9rem; color: #495057; margin: 0; line-height: 1.55; }
        .card .tag {
          display: inline-block;
          background: #e94560;
          color: white;
          padding: 0.15rem 0.6rem;
          border-radius: 20px;
          font-size: 0.75rem;
          font-weight: 700;
          margin-bottom: 0.7rem;
        }
        /* ── Install block ────────────────────────────────────── */
        .install-box {
          background: #f1f3f5;
          border-left: 4px solid #0f3460;
          border-radius: 6px;
          padding: 1.2rem 1.5rem;
          margin: 1.5rem 0;
        }
        .install-box pre { margin: 0; background: transparent; padding: 0; }
        /* ── Section heading ──────────────────────────────────── */
        h2.section-head {
          color: #0f3460;
          border-bottom: 2px solid #e94560;
          padding-bottom: 0.35rem;
          margin-top: 2.5rem;
          margin-bottom: 1.2rem;
        }
        /* ── Feature list ─────────────────────────────────────── */
        .feature-grid {
          display: grid;
          grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr));
          gap: 1rem;
          margin: 1.2rem 0 2rem;
        }
        .feature {
          display: flex;
          gap: 0.75rem;
          align-items: flex-start;
        }
        .feature .fi { font-size: 1.3rem; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 0.1rem; }
        .feature p   { margin: 0; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.5; }
        .feature strong { color: #0f3460; }
        /* ── Comparison table ─────────────────────────────────── */
        table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 0.9rem; margin: 1rem 0 2rem; }
        th { background: #0f3460; color: white; padding: 0.6rem 0.9rem; text-align: left; }
        td { padding: 0.55rem 0.9rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #dee2e6; }
        tr:hover td { background: #f8f9fa; }
        </style>
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
# No computation on this landing page
```

<!-- ── HERO ──────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<div class="hero">
<h2>🤖 Rhobots</h2>
<p>The full BERTopic pipeline — transformer embeddings, UMAP dimensionality reduction,
HDBSCAN density clustering, and class-based TF-IDF — running entirely in R.
No Python. No conda. No reticulate.</p>
<div class="badges">
  <span class="badge">📦 CRAN v0.1.6</span>
  <span class="badge">🔬 MIT License</span>
  <span class="badge">🖥️ R ≥ 4.1</span>
  <span class="badge">🪟 Windows · 🍎 macOS · 🐧 Linux</span>
</div>
</div>

<!-- ── PIPELINE ─────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">The Pipeline</h2>

<p>Rhobots implements the four-stage BERTopic pipeline as a set of modular, pluggable R objects.
Each stage can be swapped out independently — use PCA instead of UMAP, k-means instead of HDBSCAN,
or bring your own representation model.</p>

<div class="pipeline">
  <div class="pipe-step">
    <span class="icon">📝</span>
    <div class="label">Embed</div>
    <div class="pkg">torch · safetensors</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipe-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipe-step">
    <span class="icon">📉</span>
    <div class="label">Reduce</div>
    <div class="pkg">uwot · UMAP</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipe-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipe-step">
    <span class="icon">🔵</span>
    <div class="label">Cluster</div>
    <div class="pkg">Rcpp · HDBSCAN</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipe-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipe-step">
    <span class="icon">🏷️</span>
    <div class="label">Represent</div>
    <div class="pkg">c-TF-IDF · POS</div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ── RESOURCES ────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">Resources</h2>

<div class="card-grid">

<a class="card" href="tutorial.html">
  <div class="tag">Hands-on</div>
  <div class="card-icon">🔬</div>
  <h4>Full Tutorial</h4>
  <p>A complete walkthrough on 3,328 African research abstracts — from raw text to labelled topics,
  quality sweep, interactive visualisations, and LLM-generated topic names.</p>
</a>

<a class="card" href="umap_explainer.html">
  <div class="tag">How it works</div>
  <div class="card-icon">📉</div>
  <h4>How UMAP Works</h4>
  <p>Step-by-step technical explainer with illustrations: the manifold hypothesis,
  fuzzy membership weights, SGD optimisation, and the effect of every parameter.</p>
</a>

<a class="card" href="hdbscan_explainer.html">
  <div class="tag">How it works</div>
  <div class="card-icon">🔵</div>
  <h4>How HDBSCAN Works</h4>
  <p>Core distances, mutual reachability distance, Borůvka MST, the condensed cluster tree,
  EOM extraction, and what every parameter actually controls.</p>
</a>

</div>

<!-- ── WHY RHOBOTS ──────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">Why Rhobots?</h2>

<div class="feature-grid">
  <div class="feature">
    <span class="fi">🐍</span>
    <p><strong>No Python dependency.</strong> The entire pipeline — embeddings, UMAP, HDBSCAN,
    c-TF-IDF — runs in R through <code>torch</code>, <code>uwot</code>, and <code>dbscan</code>.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="feature">
    <span class="fi">🤗</span>
    <p><strong>Any Hugging Face model.</strong> Load BERT, SciBERT, BGE, E5, SPECTER2, or any
    BERT-architecture model directly from the Hub with <code>load_hf_bert()</code>.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="feature">
    <span class="fi">🔢</span>
    <p><strong>Automatic topic count.</strong> HDBSCAN finds the right number of topics for your
    data — no need to pre-specify <em>k</em>.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="feature">
    <span class="fi">🔍</span>
    <p><strong>Hyperparameter sweep.</strong> <code>sweep_topics()</code> evaluates many
    parameter combinations in one call, reusing cached embeddings.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="feature">
    <span class="fi">🌿</span>
    <p><strong>Noise-aware.</strong> Documents that don't fit any topic get label <em>−1</em>
    rather than being forced into a cluster.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="feature">
    <span class="fi">🔌</span>
    <p><strong>Pluggable architecture.</strong> Swap any stage — use PCA instead of UMAP,
    k-means instead of HDBSCAN, or a custom representation model.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ── COMPARISON TABLE ─────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">How It Compares</h2>

| | LDA (`topicmodels`) | STM (`stm`) | Rhobots |
|---|---|---|---|
| Needs Python | No | No | **No** |
| Pre-trained embeddings | No | No | **Yes** |
| Topic count specified | Yes (fixed *k*) | Yes (fixed *k*) | **Automatic** |
| Handles noise | No | No | **Yes (label −1)** |
| Non-linear structure | No | No | **Yes (UMAP + HDBSCAN)** |
| Domain-specific encoders | No | No | **Yes (any HF model)** |
| Hyperparameter sweep | No | No | **Yes** |

<!-- ── INSTALLATION ───────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">Installation</h2>

**Step 1 — Install from CRAN:**

<div class="install-box">
```r
install.packages("Rhobots")
```
</div>

**Step 2 — Install the torch backend** (once per machine, ~560 MB download):

<div class="install-box">
```r
library(Rhobots)
rhobots_install()
# Restart your R session when prompted
```
</div>

::: {.callout-note}
**Windows users** must first install the
[Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable 2022](https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x64.exe)
before running `rhobots_install()`.
:::

**Step 3 — Try the built-in demo:**

<div class="install-box">
```r
rhobots_demo()
```
</div>

The demo downloads four Gutenberg books, embeds them, sweeps parameters, fits a topic model,
and produces an interactive visualisation — no data files needed.

<!-- ── QUICK START ────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">Quick Start</h2>

```r
library(Rhobots)

# 1. Load a sentence encoder
enc <- load_hf_bert("sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2")

# 2. Embed your documents (result cached to disk)
emb <- embed_texts_cached(enc, docs, cache_file = "emb.rds", normalize = TRUE)

# 3. Sweep parameters to find the best combination
sw  <- sweep_topics(docs, emb,
                    n_neighbors  = c(10, 15, 20),
                    n_components = c(5, 10),
                    min_pts      = c(10, 20, 30))
print(sw)

# 4. Fit with the best parameters
fit <- fit_bertopic(docs, emb,
                    umap_n_neighbors  = sw$best$n_neighbors,
                    umap_n_components = sw$best$n_components,
                    hdbscan_min_pts   = sw$best$min_pts)

# 5. Inspect topics
print(fit)
visualize_barchart(fit)
visualize_topics(fit)
```

<!-- ── CITATION ───────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2 class="section-head">Citation</h2>

If you use Rhobots in research, please cite:

<div class="install-box">
```
van der Pol, J.P.G. (2026). Rhobots: BERTopic-Style Topic Modeling Without Python.
R package version 0.1.6. https://github.com/JPvdP/Rhobots
```
</div>

The algorithm is based entirely on the Python BERTopic package by
[Maarten Grootendorst](https://maartengr.github.io/BERTopic/).
